steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Ghisbain's conclusion must follow from both facts the passage establishes: bumblebees are the main research model for wild-bee declines, yet they have unique resilience traits (social behavior, dietary generalism) that set them apart from other wild-bee genera.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — predicts bumblebee population stability: the passage documents bumblebee collapse, directly contradicting this. ✗
  • Choice B — rejects using bumblebees as a model: the passage validates the modeling practice; Ghisbain questions its limits, not its legitimacy. ✗
  • Choice C — focuses on comparing environmental threats: neither text nor Ghisbain's reasoning centers on which threats are greater. ✗
  • Choice D — cautions that bumblebee responses may not apply to other wild bees: follows directly from their unique resilience traits making extrapolation unreliable. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
